items.title: 'We didn't give up': Afghan women's refugee cricketers celebrate ICC decision

children.content: Five years since fleeing Afghanistan after the Taliban returned to power, the country's women's refugee cricket team has secured a pathway to compete at the international level.

image.altText: Benafsha Hashimi, Afghanistan Women's Cricket Team

items.title: 'I didn't feel safe': Mum reveals concerns before childcare closure

children.content: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

image.altText: A mother stands with a serious expression with her two children.

items.title: These lemurs like to 'move it, move it' to the sound of a harp

children.content: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

gallery.altText: A woman playing a harp beside a lemur.

thumbnail.altText: A woman playing a harp beside a lemur.

items.title: Canberra United FC finally has a buyer. Who are the new owners?

children.content: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

image.altText: Football teammates celebrating.

items.title: Hume Highway reopens after truck driver dies in crash

children.content: A man has died after a fiery crash on the Hume Highway this morning, where two trucks collided and left part of the highway closed for hours.

image.altText: A plume of smoke behind a fire truck on a road in the dark

items.title: Canberra childcare centre closes suddenly, worker sacked

children.content: Parents at Edge Early Learning in Higgins received an email last night advising the regulator had ordered the centre to immediately shut.

items.title: ACT minister responds to claim that hospital turned NSW patient away

children.content: ACT Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says a misunderstanding led a NSW patient to believe they had been denied healthcare in Canberra because they lived over the border.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

image.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

items.title: Boys who embrace 'expansive masculinity' likely to fare better

children.content: These men are being the role models they never had. They hope to curb Australia's masculinity crisis.

gallery.altText: a young boy smiles at the camera, while hugging his dad whose face we can't see

thumbnail.altText: a young boy smiles at the camera, while hugging his dad whose face we can't see

items.title: Sara Albrecht took on Trump's tariffs. She got 'violent' calls

children.content: When a law centre decided to fight Donald Trump's "liberation day" tariffs, it was acutely aware of possible "retaliation" from a president who takes matters "personally".

image.altText: A woman stands in front of multiple microphones with two men in suits standing behind her.

items.title: As Europe follows Australia's social media ban, the US opts for own way

children.content: More and more countries are following Australia's lead and introducing social media bans for children, but US policies are piecemeal and vary from state to state.

image.altText: Alexis sits at an outdoor table with a metal fence and trees in the background.

items.title: In a secret safe at this luxury house, police struck gold

children.content: Indonesian police investigating suspected corruption discover millions of dollars worth of currency and 74 kilograms of gold in a safe hidden in a luxury house near Jakarta.

gallery.altText: Gold bars on two suitcases pictured from above.

thumbnail.altText: Gold bars on two suitcases pictured from above.

items.title: 'Lack of evidence' means Lao officials won't lay charges over teens' deaths

children.content: Investigators in Laos gave one of their first detailed accounts of the investigation into the deaths of six people, including 19-year-old Australians Holly Morton-Bowles and Bianca Jones, saying they don't have enough evidence to lay charges because autopsies were not carried out.

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: Quiet change to Nauru policing powers could thwart legal challenges

children.content: Advocates question the timing of law changes that coincide with mounting federal court challenges from individuals fighting deportation.

image.altText: A Nauru man in a black police officers shirt, stands with his hands on a desk.

items.title: A US tech start-up wants to turn night into day. The question is why?

children.content: Reflect Orbital has won initial approval for a plan to put thousands of mirrors in orbit that would be visible from anywhere on Earth. But will anyone pay for sunlight?

gallery.altText: A large circle of sunlight illuminates a city at night

thumbnail.altText: A large circle of sunlight illuminates a city at night

items.title: The Houdini of Boggo Road escaped the prison in 1946. He shouldn't have taken a friend

children.content: In 1946, Arthur 'Slim' Halliday recruited two associates, scaled the walls of Boggo Road Gaol for a second time, hopped in a taxi and vanished.

image.altText: A graphic collage of three prison escapees, a news article, and Boggo Road Gaol.

items.title: Australia's Herbert makes miracle shot but falls to fourth at The Open

children.content: Australia's Lucas Herbert makes one of the shots of The Open, but loses his day two lead to fall to fourth.

image.altText: A man swings a golf driver.

items.title: Australia's only full-time penny-farthing maker is breaking world records

children.content: Farmer-turned-professional penny-farthing maker Dan Bolwell has a cult following of international enthusiasts.

gallery.altText: Dan Bolwell in his workshop kneeling beside a penny farthing
A huge penny farthing and a tiny one, surrounded by supporters in London

thumbnail.altText: Dan Bolwell in his workshop kneeling beside a penny farthing
A huge penny farthing and a tiny one, surrounded by supporters in London

items.title: Say goodbye Norma Jean because it's Spicks and Specks quiz time

children.content: Your spelling and general trivia skills are useless here. Settle into some pop culture nostalgia.

image.altText: Adam Hills, Myf Warhurst and Alan Brough stand together smiling in front of a brick wall with Spicks and Specks posters

items.title: Like The Beatles and Beach Boys? You're going to love The Lemon Twigs

children.content: These New York twenty-somethings make music indebted to Brian Wilson, Todd Rundgren and the Fab Four. But do The Lemon Twigs get sick of the comparisons?

image.altText: White brothers with shoulder-length brown hair pose in winter coats against a wooden window frame

items.title: Eight killed, 60 wounded, as Ukraine attacks Russian warehouses

children.content: Two sprawling warehouses of Russia's major online retailer are hit by Ukrainian drones, local officials say, with massive plumes of smoke towering over a facility at Elektrostal, 50 kilometres east of Moscow.

gallery.altText: Two huge plumes of grey smoke are rising from the scene of a warehouse fire.

thumbnail.altText: Two huge plumes of grey smoke are rising from the scene of a warehouse fire.

items.title: Musos fear payday super rules could ruin live music industry

children.content: Two long-running Australian jazz festivals have been cancelled, with organisers blaming new superannuation rules.

image.altText: a man, holding an instrument, looking at the camera

items.title: How brighter, safer headlights may cause problems for other drivers

children.content: Bright LED headlights improve visibility for motorists and make them significantly safer at night, but what about the driver coming the other way?

image.altText: Glare from a new car's headlights at night.

items.title: How an Italian sculptor created the World Cup trophy that became an icon

children.content: It has become the most iconic trophy in world sport, and it all started in a small design studio in Milan more than 50 years ago.

gallery.altText: Two footballers hold a golden trophy.

thumbnail.altText: Two footballers hold a golden trophy.

items.title: 'Not particularly fond of him': McIlroy blasts American over Open drama

children.content: Golf champion Rory McIlroy takes aim at Bryson DeChambeau after the American causes a delay in tee times being released.

image.altText: A golfer looks on during a round.

items.title: Pies get home against Blues, Lions survive Eagles scare

children.content: Collingwood completes a 21-point win over rivals Carlton, as Brisbane is forced to work for a 24-point victory over West Coast.

image.altText: An AFL player in black and white raises his arms in celebration

items.title: Wallabies thrash Italy as Schmidt era ends in triumph

children.content: The Wallabies record a convincing 57-10 victory over Italy in Perth, as Joe Schmidt's coaching tenure finishes on a winning note.

image.altText: Jeremy Williams and Joe Schmidt share a laugh.

items.title: 'It's not right': Dragons fume after pushing Warriors

children.content: Second-placed New Zealand Warriors squeak to a nervy 20-12 victory over wooden-spoon favourite St George Illawarra, although the Dragons fume over narrow refereeing calls.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in red and white celebrate a try, jumping on top of eachother

items.title: Exercise Pitch Black 2026 war games take flight

children.content: Military aircraft and aviators from 20 countries land in Darwin ahead of Exercise Pitch Black 2026, with the biennial war games poised to begin.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a transport plane outline in the sunrise.

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a transport plane outline in the sunrise.

items.title: From 40 cartographers to two, street directories are nearing end of the road

children.content: While sales of the once-ubiquitous street directories have fallen dramatically since their peak in the 1980s, they still have a niche market.

image.altText: A street directory sits on a steering wheel

items.title: Bid to fix stubborn Murray River sand slug 'hamstrung' by competing demands

children.content: The Murray Darling Basin Authority is finalising its study on what to do about an increasing river impediment amid a First Nations call to find common ground.

image.altText: Aerial shot of sand beneath the water at a bend in the river

items.title: Cattle farmers plant trees for sustainable aviation fuel

children.content: A north Queensland farming family is trialling planting biofuel-producing trees alongside their cattle.

image.altText: A mother and daughter farmers standing in a field patting cattle.

items.title: Indian activist on hunger strike forcibly removed by police

children.content: Sonam Wangchuk, who began a hunger strike last month in solidarity with India's youth party after an examination scandal affected millions of students, was forced to go to hospital by court order due to his "deteriorating health condition".

image.altText: A man lies on his back against a pillow during a hunger strike while a doctor examines him.

items.title: Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times

children.content: The largest residential fire in modern Norwegian history destroys more than 100 homes near Oslo in what a local official described as "a terrible incident".

gallery.altText: Smoke rises from terraced houses on a hillside. The picture is looking down on the village which is surrounded by trees..

thumbnail.altText: Smoke rises from terraced houses on a hillside. The picture is looking down on the village which is surrounded by trees..
